_comment: Fun facts for knockout matches, keyed by match id. Each entry may have `funFacts` (array of strings) and an optional `whyItMatters` string. Every fact here is researched and sourced from real reporting — do not add invented trivia. Matches without an entry simply render no facts.

m97.whyItMatters: A rematch of the 2022 semi-final, when France ended the greatest run an African side has ever had at a World Cup.
funFacts: These teams had never met at a World Cup before their 2022 semi-final, which France won 2-0 through Théo Hernández and Randal Kolo Muani.
Morocco's run to that semi-final made them the first African and first Arab nation ever to reach the last four of a World Cup.
Morocco reached this quarter-final under Mohamed Ouahbi, beating the Netherlands on penalties after Issa Diop's late leveller, then Canada 3-0.
France are chasing a third straight World Cup final — something only one European nation has ever managed.
Achraf Hakimi and Kylian Mbappé, PSG teammates, line up on opposite sides again.

m98.whyItMatters: Forty years after Belgium knocked Spain out of a World Cup quarter-final on penalties, the two meet at exactly the same stage.
funFacts: Their only previous World Cup quarter-final, at Mexico 1986, finished 1-1 after extra time before Belgium won the shootout 5-4.
Spain took revenge four years later, beating Belgium 2-1 in the group stage at Italia '90 through Alberto Górriz and Míchel.
Spain arrived at this quarter-final without having conceded a single goal in the tournament.
Romelu Lukaku's goals in North America moved him level with Diego Maradona, Rudi Völler and Rivaldo on eight career World Cup goals.
Los Angeles Stadium, which opened in 2020, sits about half an hour from the Rose Bowl, where the 1994 World Cup final was played.

m99.whyItMatters: Norway have never reached a World Cup quarter-final before. England have reached eleven — and won only one semi-final, sixty years ago.
funFacts: Norway and England had never faced each other at a World Cup, or at any major tournament, before this meeting.
Norway knocked out Brazil 2-1 in the last 16 with an Erling Haaland brace, extending a remarkable record: Norway have never lost to Brazil in senior men's football.
Haaland arrived on seven goals in four appearances at his first World Cup, level at the top of the scoring charts with Mbappé and Messi.
Score here and Haaland would be the first European to find the net in each of his first five World Cup games since Gerd Müller in 1970.
Harry Kane last played a World Cup quarter-final in 2022, when he missed a late penalty against France.

m100.whyItMatters: Switzerland have never beaten Argentina. They also haven't reached a World Cup semi-final in their history.
funFacts: Argentina and Switzerland have met seven times and Switzerland have never won, with two friendly draws their only escapes.
Their last World Cup meeting came in the 2014 last 16, when Argentina needed extra time to win 1-0.
Switzerland reached this quarter-final by keeping Colombia out for 120 minutes and winning the shootout — their first last-eight appearance since 1954.
Argentina are trying to become the first back-to-back World Cup winners since Brazil in 1962.
Lionel Messi came into the quarter-finals at 39 years old and leading the tournament's scoring charts.
